content:
{
  "title": "Dynamic Function Signature Prompting for Enhanced Code Generation",
  "problem": "Current large language models (LLMs) often generate syntactically correct but semantically invalid code due to inadequate contextual grounding in function signatures and usage patterns. Existing prompting methods insufficiently communicate explicit API constraints, limiting code accuracy and utility.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are rapidly integrated into developer workflows, improving their precision in generating usable code is critical. API misuse and semantic errors reduce trust and usefulness, especially as codebases grow and evolve. Prompt engineering that better encodes programmatic constraints can close this gap and drive real-world adoption.",
  "method": "We propose Dynamic Function Signature Prompting (DFSP): before code generation, the prompt is automatically augmented with relevant function signature(s), including argument types and docstrings, extracted on-the-fly from available code context or documentation. A retrieval module selects the most contextually relevant signatures based on the task or code snippet to be completed. The final prompt presents these signatures in a standardized format directly before the code generation request, explicitly grounding the model in correct API usage.",
  "experiment_plan": "Evaluate DFSP on HumanEval and MBPP datasets, comparing code generation accuracy and functional correctness against GPT-4 with standard zero-shot and few-shot prompting as the strongest baselines. Metrics include exact match accuracy, pass@k, and static analysis for API misuse. Conduct ablation to measure impact of signature retrieval quality and format."
}
